notes.introduction: An exposition of John 13:1-16 on the foot-washing narrative, focusing on the exchange between Jesus and Peter (vv. 6-10) as a picture of the believer's ongoing need for spiritual cleansing, set within a pre-revival-meeting context. The Old Testament laver and priestly consecration in Exodus 29-30, along with John 17 and Ephesians 5, provide supporting framework for the central application.

sections.heading: What Revival Is and Is Not
Romans 13 and the Call to Awake
John 13: Service, Humility, and the Foot Washing
Two Words for Washing: Salvation vs. Daily Cleansing
Old Testament Type: Priestly Consecration and the Laver
Why Feet Get Dirty: Sin and Living in the World
The Remedy: Cleansing Through the Word
